analysis_result: 0/54 samples (0.0%) - DISCREPANT with paper's reported 15%. The MOESM5 MAF contains only 1 PTCH1 variant total (in sample Tumor_M2242), and it is classified as 'Silent' (synonymous). After applying the specified filters (FILTER==PASS, Hugo_Symbol=='PTCH1', non-synonymous), zero PTCH1 mutations remain, yielding 0/54 = 0.0%. This does not reproduce the paper's Fig 2b PTCH1 frequency of 15%, suggesting the oncoprint may include copy-number alterations (deletions/losses) not captured in the SNV/indel MAF, or the figure uses a different data source.
denominator_used: N = 54 (all samples in the WES cohort per MOESM4 'WES' == 'X'); note MAF contains only 52 unique Tumor_Sample_Barcodes (2 WES-labeled samples have no variants recorded in MOESM5)
assumptions_made: Non-synonymous variant classifications defined as: Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, Splice_Site, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Translation_Start_Site, Nonstop_Mutation (excluding Silent); denominator is the full 54-sample WES cohort per paper statement (samples with no variants passing filters count as non-mutated); Tumor_Sample_Barcode = 'Tumor_' + WES label convention; the 15% in Fig 2b likely includes CNV/structural alterations from a separate analysis track not present in MOESM5 SNV/indel MAF.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM4 (Clinical Data): 108 rows x 15 cols; filtered WES == 'X' -> 54 WES samples
   - MOESM5 (Supplementary Table 2, MAF): 7457 rows x 123 cols; 52 unique Tumor_Sample_Barcodes
   - Barcode convention: 'Tumor_' + WES label

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - WES cohort: 54 samples (MOESM4 WES == 'X')
   - MAF coverage: 52/54 barcodes present in MOESM5 (2 samples have no variants recorded)
   - Final denominator: 54 (full WES cohort as specified)

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - FILTER: 100% 'PASS' in MOESM5 (all 7457 rows pass QC)
   - Variant_Classification distribution: Missense_Mutation 4572, Silent 2390, Nonsense 158, Frame_Shift_Del 120, Splice_Site 85, In_Frame_Del 43, In_Frame_Ins 39, Frame_Shift_Ins 37, Translation_Start_Site 7, Nonstop_Mutation 6
   - Non-synonymous set: all above except 'Silent'

D. COMPUTATION
   - Filter 1: FILTER == 'PASS' -> 7457 rows
   - Filter 2: Hugo_Symbol == 'PTCH1' -> 1 row (sample Tumor_M2242, p.R543=, Silent)
   - Filter 3: Variant_Classification in non-synonymous set -> 0 rows
   - Unique samples with qualifying PTCH1 mutation: 0
   - Frequency: 0/54 = 0.0%

E. RESULT
   - PTCH1 non-synonymous mutation frequency: 0/54 (0.0%)
   - Paper Fig 2b reports PTCH1 at 15% (~8/54)
   - Classification: DISCREPANT

F. CAVEATS
   - Only 1 PTCH1 variant exists in the entire MAF and it is Silent (synonymous), so no filtering criteria interpretation recovers 15%.
   - The paper's 15% PTCH1 rate in Fig 2b oncoprint almost certainly reflects an 'alteration' definition that includes copy number losses/deletions (PTCH1 is a common tumor suppressor lost in MPNST), which are not captured in the SNV/indel MAF of MOESM5.
   - The 54-sample denominator is preserved per paper convention; samples without MAF entries (Tumor_Sample_Barcode absent in MOESM5) still count as non-mutated.
   - Top mutated gene by MAF is NF1 at 24/54 = 44%, consistent with MPNST biology and likely with Fig 2b's top row.
   - Unable to reconcile with paper's 15% using only MOESM5 SNV/indel data; CNV data (likely from methylation-derived CONUMEE calls) would be needed.
